message.content: The ocean appears blue primarily due to the absorption and scattering of sunlight. Water absorbs colors in the red part of the light spectrum more efficiently than it does in the blue part. As sunlight penetrates the water, the longer wavelengths (reds, oranges, and yellows) are absorbed more quickly, while the shorter wavelengths (blues) are scattered and reflected back to our eyes. This scattering effect, combined with the depth and purity of the water, enhances the blue appearance of the ocean. Additionally,
